questions.scenario: A team wants Kubernetes APIs but does not want to manage node pools directly for a normal service platform. Which GKE mode is the cleaner first conversation?

options.text: Standard only because it has more controls.

questions.explanation: Autopilot is the more managed GKE mode and is often the clearer first GKE model.

questions.scenario: A platform team needs custom node pool behavior and more infrastructure-level control. Which mode may fit better?
questions.explanation: Standard gives more direct node and infrastructure control, with more operating responsibility.

questions.scenario: Which statements about GKE mode choice are healthy?

options.text: Autopilot reduces node management for many workloads.
Standard should be chosen just because it sounds more powerful.

questions.explanation: Mode choice should come from operating needs, not reflex.
